votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking length equality, preservation of elements at odd indices, permutation of elements at even indices, and sortedness of elements at even indices.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking length equality, unchanged elements at odd indices, permutation of elements at even indices, and sortedness of elements at even indices.

The Python code correctly implements the extraction of elements at even indices and accurately translates the specification, checking length equality, unchanged elements at odd indices, permutation of even-indexed elements, and sortedness of even-indexed elements.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: (1) checks length equality, (2) checks odd-indexed elements are preserved (Coq's Nat.odd uses 0-indexing where odd indices are 1,3,5,...), (3) checks get_evens extracts even-indexed elements (0,2,4,...) which matches Coq's recursive definition, (4) checks permutation by comparing sorted lists, and (5) checks evens_res is sorted. The get_evens implementations are equivalent - Coq takes 1st element, skips 2nd, recurses on rest, which gives indices 0,2,4,... same as Python's i%2==0 check.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: (1) checks length equality, (2) verifies odd-indexed elements are unchanged (Coq's Nat.odd uses 0-indexing where odd indices are 1,3,5,...), (3) checks permutation of even-indexed elements by comparing sorted versions, and (4) checks that even-indexed elements in result are sorted. The get_evens function correctly extracts elements at indices 0,2,4,... matching the Coq recursive definition.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: (1) checks equal lengths, (2) verifies odd-indexed elements are unchanged (Coq's Nat.odd i = true means i is odd, matching Python's i % 2 == 1), (3) checks that evens from l and res are permutations by comparing sorted versions, (4) checks evens_res is sorted. The get_evens function correctly extracts even-indexed elements (0, 2, 4, ...) matching Coq's recursive definition that takes the first element, skips the second, and recurses.

Both define even-indexed elements identically and check: (1) same length, (2) odd indices unchanged, (3) even-indexed multiset preserved (via sorted equality), and (4) even-indexed elements in res are nondecreasing. These match the Coq spec’s concrete behavior for all inputs.

Python get_evens selects 0,2,4,... indices like Coq. sort_even_spec enforces equal lengths, odd indices unchanged, even-indexed elements are a permutation (via sorted equality) and nondecreasing (evens_res == sorted(evens_res)), matching the Coq Permutation and Sorted Z.le conditions.

Python enforces same length, preserves all odd-index elements, and ensures the even-index elements of res are a permutation of those of l and sorted nondecreasing; get_evens matches Coq’s even-index extraction. Comparing sorted lists correctly checks permutation for integers.
